purpose: Reusable debugging context for agents injection runtime/load failures.
Avoids re-discovering PRD constraints, injection wiring, package entrypoints, and known PoC limitations.
applies_to: Gem Team agents injection in OpenCode plugin config hook
non_goals: No upstream Gem Team agent behavior redesign.

tech_stack.name: TypeScript
tech_stack.usage_context: ESM plugin source compiled to dist/src for package entrypoints.

tech_stack.name: OpenCode plugin SDK
tech_stack.usage_context: PluginModule default export with server hooks; config hook injects agents.

tech_stack.name: node:test
tech_stack.usage_context: Unit/integration-style tests compiled then run from dist/test/**/*.test.js.

conventions: Use npm run compile; package entrypoints target ./dist/src/index.js.
npm test runs compile first and then node --test dist/test/**/*.test.js.
docs/PRD.md is product source of truth; AGENTS.md is terse agent-facing repo guidance.
Generated agent assets live under agents/generated and must remain aligned with manifest hashes.

hard: Do not add role_models, agent_complexity_models, per-agent model overrides, provider-side routing, or concrete model IDs.
Do not override existing user agent.model during generated default injection.
Do not let gem-orchestrator route to itself.
Do not claim GitHub remote install success unless actually verified end-to-end.
T2 minimal fix budget is <=300 changed lines and may only touch files T1 proves necessary; otherwise replan.

T3 CLI regression must run with isolated temp HOME/USERPROFILE, XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME, and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR or current OpenCode equivalent; never read or mutate user/global OpenCode config.
soft: Prefer minimal additive tests/validation over invasive rewrites.
Keep README/AGENTS/docs aligned only if behavior or verified install evidence changes.
compatibility: OpenCode package target detection requires main or exports ./server to resolve a server target; current package points both to dist/src/index.js.
Generated loader searches process.cwd()/agents/generated and paths relative to compiled module for package installs.
security_requirements: Do not read, print, or persist API keys/tokens/cookies/private keys.
Run OpenCode CLI verification in isolated temp fixtures; avoid global config mutation.

src/: Plugin source, hooks, generated loader, sync validation.
agents/generated/: Synced Gem Team agent markdown bodies and manifest.json.
test/: Node test coverage for plugin config injection and sync/package behavior.
scripts/: Sync and validation scripts including dist/package checks.
docs/: PRD, installation PoC evidence, and plan artifacts.

patterns: Plugin default export satisfies PluginModule with id and async server function.
server config hook mutates OpenCode config by calling injectGemTeamAgents(config).
Injection creates config.agent if missing, then writes 16 generated agents by canonical slug.
Existing same-slug user config is merged over generated defaults via {...generated, ...existing}.

why_relevant: If OpenCode loads this module, config hook calls injectGemTeamAgents.

key_elements.description: Returns Hooks with config async callback that calls injectGemTeamAgents(config).

why_relevant: Direct function writes generated agents into config.agent and preserves existing same-slug fields.

key_elements.description: Iterates getGeneratedGemTeamAgents and writes base description/mode/prompt; existing configs are merged over generated defaults.
Uses object spread order {...generated, ...existing}; preserves user prompt/mode/model and arbitrary fields.

purpose: Runtime loading of generated manifest and agent markdown assets.
why_relevant: A package/install path issue here can make OpenCode load fail even though direct source tests pass.

key_elements.description: Searches process.cwd()/agents/generated and module-relative candidates; throws if asset not found.
Validates manifest, reads 16 canonical agent prompts, checks prompt hashes.

purpose: Runtime package entrypoints and validation commands.
why_relevant: OpenCode package-style loading uses dist/src/index.js via main/exports; stale/missing dist can make injection appear ineffective.

key_elements.description: Both package root and ./server export point to ./dist/src/index.js with declarations.
compile/typecheck/test/lint/sync/validate commands; no build script by design.

purpose: Existing injection tests and regression gap.
why_relevant: Tests prove direct source hook works but do not fully prove real OpenCode CLI/cache install path.

key_elements.element: Gem Team config hook injection suite
key_elements.description: Covers 16-agent direct injection, plugin.server config hook, model preservation, no default model field, no orchestrator self target, prompt hash match.

why_relevant: Records verified local path install, pending GitHub remote source, dist strategy, and safe install boundaries.

key_elements.description: Local package-path install and agent visibility verified; GitHub remote install pending.
Only npm run compile + local path plugin install is verified; observed GitHub syntax is not final proof.

patterns_found.name: Generated defaults under existing user config
Package entrypoint depends on compiled dist
Local path install verified, GitHub source pending

internal: test/plugin-config.test.ts imports src modules directly, then npm test compiles and runs dist/test
external: OpenCode CLI for package-path install and agent list verification

gotchas.text: Existing same-slug user agent config can mask generated prompt/mode by design; this is not automatically a bug because PRD requires preserving user agent.model and existing config fields.
Direct unit tests can pass while OpenCode loads stale or missing dist/src/index.js in package-style installs.
Generated asset lookup can fail in installed/cache layouts if agents/generated is not present at expected package-relative paths.
Plan review requires T2 to stay atomic: <=300 changed lines and only T1-proven necessary files.
CLI validation evidence is only acceptable when executed under temporary config/home/cache paths and with redacted output.

security_considerations.concern: Use isolated temp HOME/USERPROFILE, XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_CACHE_HOME, and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR or OpenCode equivalent; avoid reading/printing environment secrets or mutating global OpenCode config.

testing_patterns.test_organization: Source tests under test/ compile to dist/test and run via npm test.

domain_context.error_handling: generated-loader throws explicit errors for invalid manifest, missing slug, missing generated asset, or prompt hash mismatch.
domain_context.data_flow: OpenCode loads package entry dist/src/index.js -> plugin.server returns config hook -> hook mutates config.agent -> generated-loader reads manifest/prompts -> OpenCode sees gem-* agents.

open_questions.question: Which runtime segment caused the user's observed injection failure?
open_questions.context: Current evidence supports direct injection but not necessarily installed/cached OpenCode load path.

prior_decisions.decision: Preserve existing same-slug agent fields over generated defaults.
rationale: PRD requires not overriding existing OpenCode agent.model
Existing tests assert model, prompt, mode, permission, and arbitrary user fields are preserved
linked_constraints: Do not override existing user agent.model
linked_patterns: Generated defaults under existing user config

prior_decisions.decision: Use compile/dist package entrypoints instead of build/prep lifecycle scripts.
rationale: docs record build script was renamed compile to avoid git-dependency preparation trigger
linked_patterns: Package entrypoint depends on compiled dist

do_not_re_read: docs/PRD.md unless acceptance criteria changed or contradiction appears.
safe_to_assume: injectGemTeamAgents direct invocation currently injects 16 agents in source-level tests.
Existing user same-slug config preservation is intentional and PRD-backed.
GitHub remote install/load remains unverified unless a later task records new end-to-end evidence.
verify_before_use: Whether dist/src is current after any source edits; run npm run compile and validate:dist.
Whether opencode CLI exists and which version is being used before CLI regression.
Whether agents/generated assets are present in any installed/cache package layout under test.
Whether T2 changed_files and changed_lines_count remain within the <=300 line budget and T1-proven file scope.
Whether T3 evidence includes the exact temporary HOME/config/cache paths and confirms no user/global OpenCode config access or mutation.
